economic_notes: Nation has oil and gas revenue, commercial operations, and BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing. Equity accounted investees include Kihew-Sas GP Ltd. (20%), Tse K'Wa Heritage Society (33.33%), and Six Nations Ventures Limited Partnership (16.67%). Potentially contaminated land parcel owned by Prophet River Holdings Ltd. Site C settlement of $4,000,000 received in prior year 2021.
notes: Fiscal year is 2022 (year ended March 31, 2022). Audit report signed July 29, 2024 — unusually late. Budget was not formally approved by Chief and Council. Nine OGOs consolidated using full consolidation method; two GBEs and one GBP accounted for using modified equity method. Prophet River First Nation Trust is a major revenue source providing $10.95M. Wages and benefits in Housing segment are negative (-$38,091) due to allocations. Prior year (2021) included a $4,000,000 Site C settlement in Other revenue which was a one-time item. Distribution to members payable of $1,992,306 relates to Site C settlement paid out. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $3,398,041 + CMHC $25,580 + FNHC $552,516 + FNESC $271,421 + BC $711,476 + Treaty 8 Heritage Trust $800,000 = $5,759,034 (note: Treaty 8 Heritage Trust classification uncertain). osr = total revenue minus government transfers.
